Home»Sport»Paris Olympics 2024»Olympics: Athletes forced to sleep on ‘anti-sex’ cardboard beds

The sustainable anti-sex cardboard beds are being used in the Olympic Village for the 2024 Olympics in Paris.

Athletes at this year’s Olympics have taken to social media to reveal the controversial single, anti-sex, high-resistance lightweight cardboard beds they have to sleep on.

With this Olympic Games around the corner and ready to kick off, all the attention has turned to the Olympic Village, where athletes are housed during the prestigious event. More specifically, the ‘anti-sex’ beds that are made from cardboard.

Social media is abuzz after athletes from various countries gave their millions of followers across different platforms a glimpse into their respective rooms and in particular a look at their cardboard beds.

Sevensrugbystar Libbie Janse van Rensburg, who is a member of Team South Africa at the Olympics have viewers the inside scoop: “What’s the deal with the beds in the Olympic Village? Here’s the scoop,” she said on TikTok.

What’s the deal with the beds in the Olympic Village? Here’s the scoop👀#fyp#Olympics2024#olympicvillage#olympicbeds#cardboardeds

“Just pulled up to the village and had to run it back up yall,” wrote South Africa’s skateboarding sensation Brandon Valjalo, who shared the video below with his social media followers.

Just pulled up to the village and had to run it back up yall!#olympics#paris2024#brandonvaljalo

“Ok these beds have got out of control,” wrote New Zealand swimmer Lewis Clarebut, accompanied by the video below.

Ok these beds have got out of control!#paris2024#olympics

Australian Olympic water polo player Tilly Kearns went as far as to give an in-depth review of the beds. “Already had a massage to undo the damage,” she said.

Already had a massage to undo the damage

Harry McNulty, the Irish sevens rugby, also gave his view on the cardboard bed saga: “I’ve had 0 issues with the beds/sleep.”

Replying to @jlh ive had 0 issues with the beds/sleep!

Australian tennis players Daria Saville and Ellen Perez also posted a video on TikTok of themselves ‘testing out’ the beds with a series of amusing activities, including squat jumping and doing the worm.

Testing out the cardboard beds at the Olympic Village @Ilona Maher#AllezAUS#Paris2024#Olympics#Tennis#ParisOlympics2024#ParisOlympics#olympics2024

These viral posts from athletes inside the Olympic Village sent social media into a frenzy, with many social poking fun at the cardboard beds.

“These are the fittest young people in the world; sure as hell they’ll find a way,” one person joked.

“The Olympics village always looks so cheap. Tiny rooms in cramped apartments with cardboard beds and one crap open storage unit. And one bathroom between 4 rooms I doubt the likes of Nadal will be staying in the village.”

“Village looks cosy and calm fr. Might just join the Olympics.“

Another added: “Imagine thinking that a small, narrow bed is going to stop these superhumans from doing whatever they want.”

“Athletes uploading their cardboard beds in Paris at the Olympic village is so? what happened to Olympics budget?,” quipped another person.

“Do people not remember the cardboard beds for Tokyo Olympics? I’m not shocked it’s cardboard beds again. I’ll be shocked if they stop the cardboard beds, tbh.”

Olympic chiefs have denied the anti-sex rumours and instead given a different reason behind the cardboard beds.

In an interview withReutersOlympic organizers said: “We know the media has had a lot of fun with this story but for 2024 Paris Olympics, the choice of these beds for the Olympic & Paralympic Village is primarily linked to a wider ambition to ensure minimal environmental impact and a second life for all equipment used during the short period of the Games.”

The opening ceremony of the 2024 Summer Olympics is scheduled to take place on 26 July 2024.

title: Olympics: Athletes sleeping on anti-sex cardboard beds
author: Mariette Adams
description: The sustainable anti-sex cardboard beds are being used in the Olympic Village for the 2024 Olympics in Paris.
